MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Daniel Cunningham.

Mr. Schultz requested a 30-day continuance stating that he has the schedules and that the petition will be filed in the special needs trust case (PTR-18-301830). Status on filing of the first account by the Trustee is continued to August 14, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Mar-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Jocelyn Jackson (Successor Trustee).
Franchesca Callejo for Gregory Jackson (Beneficiary) and Mariya Jackson (Trustee of Gregory's Trust).

Ms. Callejo stated that she is not objecting, that her Clients would like distribution to be ordered today, and that her Client is in urgent need of obtaining alternate housing and needs funds. Mr. Schultz stated that he filed consents from all of the Beneficiaries with regard to the requested Trustee's fees. The Fifth Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

The sale was confirmed at $875,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed July 12, 2018. MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for and with Mariya Jackson.

Ms. Callejo stated that her Client signed a declaration on Friday which was sent in for e-filing, that at the time of Mr. Jackson's death 1942 Lane Street was in the Trust, and that the Court approved a petition to list and sell the property and distribute the proceeds to permit Gregory and Sean to vacate the premises. Ms. Callejo also stated that her Client has received $43,500.00 for the relocating process, that Gregory Jackson vacated the property on June 5th, that her Client is gainfully employed and does not receive any public benefits, that the goal is to get the property sold and that she does not know how much it will sell for because it is not in the best of shape. In addition, Ms. Callejo stated that the subtrust was established on May 3rd for Gregory to receive the $43,500.00, that an account is open in the name of the subtrust, and that Gregory has asked that no bond be ordered and that the Trust not be brought under Court supervision. Ms. Jackson stated that Gregory has vacated the property, that Sean will vacate on Thursday, and that Sean will be temporarily living with her Father for up to three months to find a home that can provide the care he needs. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee, etc. is taken under submission. Ms. Callejo provided a revised order in open Court.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Revoke Powers Of Trustee, Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision, And Confirm Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-06-2011 Continued To Feb-28-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-10-294032 (Conservatorship of J.B. Jackson).

Appearances: Court-Appointed Counsel Arthur Park for and with J.B. Jackson (Conservatee). Andrew Schultz for and with Jocelyn Jackson (Granddaughter/Temporary Conservator). Gregory Jackson (Son).

Mr. Park stated that mediation was set up with Gregory O'Keefe, that the parties met prior to mediation, that an agreement was reached, and that his client had no clear objection to the appointment. J.B. Jackson, Jocelyn Jackson, and Gregory Jackson all addressed the Court. Mr. Schultz requested an allowance up to $100.00 per month for the Conservatee. The Court stated that Jocelyn Jackson will now have the legal obligation to the welfare of J.B. Jackson and granted the petition appointing Jocelyn Jackson as the Conservator of the Person and Estate of J.B. Jackson (order signed in open court).

Further orders (per written order):
1. Compliance Status Hearing - April 20, 2011, 9:00a.m., Dept. 204.
2. Assessment Fee - $1200 is charged.
3. The Certificate of Completion of the conservatorship class shall be filed by 7/6/11.
4. First Account & Status Report - filed one week before March 14, 2012.
5. An allowance of up to $100.00 per month is granted. Bond is set at $130,000.00. Mr. Park is to remain on six months.

The Court addressed the Trust stating that there is a service issue with regard to Gregory Jackson's 15 year old Son. Discussions regarding service were held. Mr. Schultz is to serve an appropriate adult at the Valencia address or someone higher up who is responsible. The Court continued the Trust matter only to 2/28/11 at 9:00 a.m., extended the temp to the same date, and directed Mr. Schultz to prep the order extending the temp.

Jocelyn Jackson viewed the required video.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Court Investigator: Probate Director Mary Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
